cards.grammarNote: The polite speech level (해요체). Add -아요 after stems with ㅏ or ㅗ vowel; -어요 elsewhere. The most common ending in everyday Korean conversation.
하다 (to do) → 해요. 하다 verbs are irregular: the stem 하 becomes 해 in -아요/어요 form. Very common in compound verbs: 공부해요 (study), 일해요 (work).
모르다 (to not know) → 몰라요. ㄹ-irregular verb: drop 르, add ㄹ + 아요. Very common: 몰라요 = I don't know.
알다 (to know) → 알아요. ㄹ verbs drop ㄹ before consonant endings but keep it before vowel endings: 알아요. 몰라요 = I don't know.
가다 (to go) → 가요. 가 has vowel ㅏ → -아요, but 가 + 아요 = 가요 (vowel drops). Very common daily verb.
오다 (to come) → 와요. 오 is irregular: 오 + 아요 = 와요 (vowel contraction).
있다 (to exist/have) → 있어요. 있 ends in consonant with non-ㅏ/ㅗ vowel → -어요. 없어요 = there isn't / I don't have.
좋다 (to be good/nice) → 좋아요. 좋 has ㅗ vowel → -아요. 좋아요 also means 'I like it'.
있어요 with rising intonation = a yes/no question. 지금 = now, 시간 = time. No question marker needed.
보다 (to see/look) → 봐요. 보 + 아요 = 봐요 (vowel contraction). 봐요 is very common in directions: 봐요 = look at this.
듣다 (to listen) → 들어요. ㄷ-irregular: ㄷ changes to ㄹ before vowel endings. 들어요 = I listen/hear.
감사하다 (to be thankful) → 감사해요. 하다 verb → 해요. 감사합니다 is the formal version.
